title: poa cenisia subsp. arctica
intro: Poa cenisia subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic meadow-grass, is a subspecies of grass that is native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Poa cenisia subsp. arctica include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Poa cenisia subsp. arctica include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or using them in salves and creams.
